title: Kwa Police take compulsory school attendance seriously
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KWANONQABA NEWS -  In an effort to raise awareness of parents’ legal obligation to ensure their children attend school, the KwaNonqaba Police’s Social Crime Office embarked on a door-to-door initiative, known as the Back to School Campaign, in the KwaNonqaba community on Wednesday 7 January.","html":"KWANONQABA NEWS - In an effort to raise awareness of parents’ legal obligation to ensure their children attend school, the KwaNonqaba Police’s Social Crime Office embarked on a door-to-door initiative, known as the Back to School Campaign, in the KwaNonqaba community on Wednesday 7 January."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"They visited homes in the Civic Park and Highway Park areas."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The South African Schools Act of 1996 requires all children between the ages of seven and 15 to attend school.","html":"The South African Schools Act of 1996 requires all children between the ages of seven and 15 to attend school."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Warrant Officer Wilson Potongwana, the KwaNonqaba Police Station’s domestic violence co-ordinator, said children loitering in the street when they are meant to be in school could lead to experimenting in acts of crime.","html":"Warrant Officer Wilson Potongwana, the KwaNonqaba Police Station’s domestic violence co-ordinator, said children loitering in the street when they are meant to be in school could lead to experimenting in acts of crime."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The KwaNonqaba Police Station hosts the Back to School Campaign every year, before the schools open, in an effort to curb the chances of this occurring.","html":"The KwaNonqaba Police Station hosts the Back to School Campaign every year, before the schools open, in an effort to curb the chances of this occurring."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The KwaNonqaba Police management lauded Marais (left), who led this year’s campaign.","html":" The KwaNonqaba Police management lauded Marais (left), who led this year’s campaign. "},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Collaborating with the Genesis Community Project and the Closing the Gap group, the initiative also focused on raising awareness about HIV and TB in the community.","html":"Collaborating with the Genesis Community Project and the Closing the Gap group, the initiative also focused on raising awareness about HIV and TB in the community."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Sergeant Salome Marais of the KwaNonqaba Police led the campaign and took the opportunity to address gender-based violence and femicide with some of the men they came across."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The KwaNonqaba Police’s management lauded her efforts. Schools in Mossel Bay and across South Africa will open on Wednesday 14 January.","html":"The KwaNonqaba Police’s management lauded her efforts. Schools in Mossel Bay and across South Africa will open on Wednesday 14 January."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
